rights: Protection against hazardous goods/services
Right to be informed about product quality, quantity, price
Access to a variety of goods/services at competitive prices
Right to be heard in consumer forums
Redressal against unfair trade practices
Right to consumer awareness
provisions: Establishment of Central Consumer Protection Authority
Establishment of Consumer Disputes Redressal Commissions (District, State, National)
Provisions for mediation and consumer mediation cells
Rules for product liability actions
Measures to prevent unfair trade practices in e-commerce and direct selling
Definition of consumer, complaint, and consumer dispute
Powers and functions of the Central Authority including investigation and recall of goods
Procedures for filing and adjudicating complaints
Appeals process for consumer disputes
Penalties for non-compliance with directions and false advertisements
penalties: Penalties for false or misleading advertisements
Punishment for manufacturing, storing, selling, or distributing adulterated products
Punishment for manufacturing, storing, selling, or distributing spurious goods
Penalty for non-compliance of directions from the Central Authority
Penalty for non-compliance of orders from Consumer Disputes Redressal Commissions
Fines and imprisonment for certain offences
beneficiaries: Legal heirs or representatives of deceased consumers
Parents or legal guardians of minor consumers

rights: Right to take input tax credit
Right to claim refund of excess tax paid
Right to appeal against decisions of authorities
Right to seek advance ruling on tax matters
Right to receive goods or services as per contract
Right to be informed of tax liabilities
Right to access business premises for inspection
Right to be heard before penalty imposition
provisions: Levy and collection of GST on intra-state supply
Registration of taxpayers
Issuance of tax invoices, credit, and debit notes
Maintenance of accounts and records
Furnishing of returns and payment of tax
Provisions for input tax credit (ITC)
Assessment and audit procedures
Procedures for refunds
Offences and penalties for non-compliance
Transitional provisions for existing taxpayers
penalties: Penalty for certain offences (e.g., evasion, false representation)
General penalty for contravention of provisions
Penalty for failure to register machines
Penalty for failure to furnish information returns
Fine for failure to furnish statistics
Detention, seizure, and release of goods and conveyances
Confiscation of goods or conveyances and levy of penalty
Punishment for certain offences (imprisonment, fine)
Late fee for delayed payment of tax
Interest on delayed payment of tax
beneficiaries: Consumers (through potential reduction in cascading taxes)
State Governments (through GST compensation and shared revenue)

rights: Right to legal recognition of electronic records and signatures
Right to use electronic records and signatures in government services
Right to retain electronic records
Right to audit documents in electronic form
Right to have contracts formed through electronic means recognized
Right to attribution, acknowledgment, and timely receipt of electronic records
Right to secure electronic records and signatures
Right to appeal decisions of adjudicating officers
Right to legal representation before the Appellate Tribunal
Right to protection of actions taken in good faith
provisions: Legal recognition of electronic records and signatures
Use of electronic records and signatures in government and its agencies
Attribution, acknowledgment, and despatch of electronic records
Secure electronic records and signatures
Regulation of Certifying Authorities
Duties of subscribers of electronic signature certificates
Penalties and adjudication for computer-related offenses
Establishment of an Appellate Tribunal for appeals
Exemption from liability for intermediaries in certain cases
Act to have overriding effect over other laws
penalties: Penalty and compensation for damage to computer, computer system, etc.
Compensation for failure to protect data
Penalty for failure to furnish information, return, etc.
Residuary penalty for contraventions
Punishment for tampering with computer source documents
Punishment for computer-related offenses
Punishment for dishonestly receiving stolen computer resource or communication device
Punishment for identity theft
Punishment for cheating by personation using a computer resource
Punishment for violation of privacy
beneficiaries: Government agencies utilizing electronic records and services
Users of computer systems and networks
The general public benefiting from secure electronic transactions
The Indian economy through promotion of digital transactions
All persons involved in or affected by electronic records and communications

rights: Right to privacy and confidentiality for children.
Right to protection from sexual assault, harassment, and pornography.
Right to have best interests and well-being considered paramount.
Right to assistance of experts during legal proceedings.
Right to legal practitioner assistance.
Right to have statements recorded in a child-friendly manner.
Right to not see the accused while testifying.
Right to have trials conducted in camera.
provisions: Defines 'child' as any person below eighteen years.
Establishes definitions for various sexual offenses against children.
Outlines punishments for penetrative sexual assault, aggravated penetrative sexual assault, sexual assault, aggravated sexual assault, and sexual harassment.
Addresses the use of children for pornographic purposes and related punishments.
Covers abetment and attempt to commit sexual offenses against children.
Mandates reporting of sexual offenses against children.
Specifies procedures for recording statements of child victims.
Designates Special Courts for trial of sexual offenses against children.
Provides for Special Public Prosecutors.
Sets timelines for recording evidence and disposal of cases involving children.
penalties: Imprisonment for penetrative sexual assault (minimum 10 years, up to life).
Imprisonment for penetrative sexual assault on a child below 16 (minimum 20 years, up to life).
Fine to be paid to the victim for medical expenses and rehabilitation.
Severe penalties for aggravated penetrative sexual assault, including life imprisonment.
Punishments for sexual assault and aggravated sexual assault.
Punishments for sexual harassment.
Punishments for using a child for pornographic purposes.
Punishments for storing child pornography.
Punishments for abetment and attempt to commit offenses.
Punishment for failure to report or for false complaints.
beneficiaries: Children below the age of eighteen years.
Society at large by preventing heinous crimes against children.
Law enforcement agencies involved in child protection.
Media, studios, and photographic facilities (through obligation to report).

rights: Right to access information from public authorities
Right to inspect documents and records
Right to take notes and extracts from documents
Right to obtain certified copies of documents
Right to obtain samples of material
Right to receive information in electronic format
Right to receive reasons for administrative decisions
provisions: Public authorities must maintain cataloged and indexed records
Public authorities must publish organizational details, functions, and duties
Public authorities must publish information on officers, employees, and their remuneration
Public authorities must publish budget allocations and expenditure details
Public authorities must publish details of subsidy beneficiaries
Public authorities must provide reasons for administrative or quasi-judicial decisions
Information should be disseminated widely and easily accessible
Public authorities must designate Public Information Officers
penalties: Imposition of penalties on Public Information Officers for non-compliance
Daily penalty of ₹250 for refusal to receive an application, malafide denial, or obstruction
Maximum penalty of ₹25,000 for repeated offenses
Penalty may be increased if the delay in providing information is without reasonable cause
Deduction from the salary of the Public Information Officer for penalties
Inquiry into the conduct of the Public Information Officer
beneficiaries: Individuals seeking accountability from public authorities
Members of the public in relation to policy formulation
